target.title: docs(memory): align descriptors and docs with recursive memory/**/*.md
target.body:
## Summary
- The runtime already walks `memory/` recursively via `collectMemoryFilesFromDir` (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.ts`), so nested files like `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md` are indexed and searched. But several agent-facing descriptors and public docs still said `memory/*.md`, which can mislead agents into thinking the index is flat.
- This PR updates the remaining stale references to `memory/**/*.md`. No runtime behavior change.

## Files touched
- `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` — lazy `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptors
- `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` — concrete `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptions
- `extensions/memory-core/src/prompt-section.ts` — system-prompt recall guidance
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — `agents.defaults.memorySearch` help text
- `docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md`, `memory.md`, `system-prompt.md`
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`, `docs/reference/token-use.md`
- `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` regenerated via `pnpm config:docs:gen`

## Note
This supersedes #72748. That PR also carried QMD watcher changes that were independently resolved upstream by #81802 (`watch-settle.ts`), so the surviving value was just this wording alignment. Splitting it out as a small, behavior-neutral PR for easier review.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent-facing descriptors and public docs said `memory/*.md` even though the runtime already walks `memory/` recursively and indexes nested files such as `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. This PR aligns the wording to `memory/**/*.md` so agents and readers see what is actually indexed. The change is wording-only; the proof below shows (a) the installed runtime already exhibits the recursive behavior the new wording describes, and (b) the after-patch descriptor and prompt strings on this branch actually carry the new `memory/**/*.md` wording when imported at runtime.

**Real environment tested**: Installed OpenClaw `2026.5.20` (npm global, current `latest` published build, ahead of the `acf265d` base this PR branches from), against the live workspace `~/.openclaw/workspace-moku` used by the gateway. Agent `main`, local embedding provider, per-agent SQLite memory store at `~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite`. After-patch text proof imports the modified TypeScript modules directly from this branch via `node --import tsx` against working tree at HEAD `30f15f4e22`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

Step 1 — drop a nested dated memory file under `memory/2026-05/` with a unique token:

```
cat > ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku/memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md <<'EOF'
Recursive memory search proof.
Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
EOF
```

Step 2 — reindex the live agent's memory store:

```
openclaw memory index
```

Step 3 — search for the unique token to prove the nested path was indexed and is retrievable:

```
openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
```

Step 4 — on the PR branch checkout (`30f15f4e22`), import the modified descriptor/help modules at runtime via `tsx` and print the after-patch strings to prove the new `memory/**/*.md` wording is observable from a real runtime call site (not just the diff):

```
node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs   # imports FIELD_HELP and buildPromptSection from this branch
```

**After-fix evidence**: Terminal output from the installed `openclaw 2026.5.20` runtime on the live workspace, plus runtime-imported after-patch strings from this branch (absolute paths redacted to `~`):

```
$ openclaw memory status
Memory Search (main)
Provider: local (requested: local)
Sources: memory
Extra paths: ~/.openclaw/shared-memory
Indexed: 105/105 files . 933 chunks
Workspace: ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku
By source:
  memory . 105/105 files . 933 chunks
```

```
$ openclaw memory index
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
Memory index updated (main).
```

```
$ openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
0.812 memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md:1-9
    Recursive memory search proof.
    Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
    This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
    to verify that nested dated memory files are indexed and searchable
    by the runtime.

0.450 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md:1-6
0.432 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/pr-draft.md:1-32
```

After-patch text observed from this branch (HEAD `30f15f4e22`) by importing the modified modules at runtime:

```
$ node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs
=== schema.help.ts: FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch'] ===
Vector search over MEMORY.md and memory/**/*.md (per-agent overrides supported).

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search + memory_get }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts; then use memory_get to pull only the needed lines. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search only }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts and answer from the matching results. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== extensions/memory-core/index.ts (post-patch source lines) ===
 127      description:
 128        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 139      description:
 140        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",

=== extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts (post-patch source lines) ===
 240      description:
 241        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 410      description:
 411        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",
```

**Observed result after fix**: (a) The freshly written nested file `memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md` (two levels deep under `memory/`) was indexed by `openclaw memory index` and returned as the top match (score `0.812`) for the unique token. A second nested hit from `memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md` (three levels deep) confirms recursion is not limited to one level. (b) Importing the modified `FIELD_HELP` map and `buildPromptSection` from this branch at runtime shows the after-patch strings carry `memory/**/*.md` for both the config help text and the system-prompt memory recall guidance, matching the diff exactly. (c) The two `memory_search` / `memory_get` description strings in `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` and `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` are inline string literals; the after-patch lines printed from this branch show the new wording in place. No runtime code paths are modified, only the descriptor and doc strings plus the regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.

**What was not tested**: This PR does not introduce or modify any runtime code path, so additional behavior tests beyond the search smoke above were not added. The recursive walk itself (`collectMemoryFilesFromDir`) is already covered by upstream tests under `packages/memory-host-sdk/`.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:gen` regenerates baseline cleanly (no other drift)
- [x] Nested memory smoke search on installed `2026.5.20` returns the new nested file at score `0.812`
- [x] On-branch runtime import shows `FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch']`, `buildPromptSection(...)` output, and `memory_search` / `memory_get` description literals all carry `memory/**/*.md`
- [ ] CI: lint / type / config-baseline check

This PR aligns the wording to `memory/**/*.md` so agents and readers see what is actually indexed. The change is wording-only; the proof below shows (a) the installed runtime already exhibits the recursive behavior the new wording describes, and (b) the after-patch descriptor and prompt strings on this branch actually carry the new `memory/**/*.md` wording when imported at runtime.

**Real environment tested**: Installed OpenClaw `2026.5.20` (npm global, current `latest` published build, ahead of the `acf265d` base this PR branches from), against the live workspace `~/.openclaw/workspace-moku` used by the gateway. Agent `main`, local embedding provider, per-agent SQLite memory store at `~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite`. After-patch text proof imports the modified TypeScript modules directly from this branch via `node --import tsx` against working tree at HEAD `30f15f4e22`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

Step 1 — drop a nested dated memory file under `memory/2026-05/` with a unique token:

```
cat > ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku/memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md <<'EOF'
Recursive memory search proof.
Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
EOF
```

Step 2 — reindex the live agent's memory store:

```
openclaw memory index
```

Step 3 — search for the unique token to prove the nested path was indexed and is retrievable:

```
openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
```

Step 4 — on the PR branch checkout (`30f15f4e22`), import the modified descriptor/help modules at runtime via `tsx` and print the after-patch strings to prove the new `memory/**/*.md` wording is observable from a real runtime call site (not just the diff):

```
node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs   # imports FIELD_HELP and buildPromptSection from this branch
```

**After-fix evidence**: Terminal output from the installed `openclaw 2026.5.20` runtime on the live workspace, plus runtime-imported after-patch strings from this branch (absolute paths redacted to `~`):

```
$ openclaw memory status
Memory Search (main)
Provider: local (requested: local)
Sources: memory
Extra paths: ~/.openclaw/shared-memory
Indexed: 105/105 files . 933 chunks
Workspace: ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku
By source:
  memory . 105/105 files . 933 chunks
```

```
$ openclaw memory index
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
Memory index updated (main).
```

```
$ openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
0.812 memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md:1-9
    Recursive memory search proof.
    Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
    This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
    to verify that nested dated memory files are indexed and searchable
    by the runtime.

0.450 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md:1-6
0.432 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/pr-draft.md:1-32
```

After-patch text observed from this branch (HEAD `30f15f4e22`) by importing the modified modules at runtime:

```
$ node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs
=== schema.help.ts: FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch'] ===
Vector search over MEMORY.md and memory/**/*.md (per-agent overrides supported).

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search + memory_get }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts; then use memory_get to pull only the needed lines. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search only }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts and answer from the matching results. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== extensions/memory-core/index.ts (post-patch source lines) ===

 128        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 139      description:
 140        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",

=== extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts (post-patch source lines) ===

 241        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 410      description:
 411        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",
```

**Observed result after fix**: (a) The freshly written nested file `memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md` (two levels deep under `memory/`) was indexed by `openclaw memory index` and returned as the top match (score `0.812`) for the unique token. A second nested hit from `memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md` (three levels deep) confirms recursion is not limited to one level. (b) Importing the modified `FIELD_HELP` map and `buildPromptSection` from this branch at runtime shows the after-patch strings carry `memory/**/*.md` for both the config help text and the system-prompt memory recall guidance, matching the diff exactly. (c) The two `memory_search` / `memory_get` description strings in `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` and `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` are inline string literals; the after-patch lines printed from this branch show the new wording in place. No runtime code paths are modified, only the descriptor and doc strings plus the regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.

**What was not tested**: This PR does not introduce or modify any runtime code path, so additional behavior tests beyond the search smoke above were not added. The recursive walk itself (`collectMemoryFilesFromDir`) is already covered by upstream tests under `packages/memory-host-sdk/`.

## Test plan

target.title: fix(ui): handle empty strings with minLength constraint in config save
target.body:
Fixes #85831

## Summary

- Modified `coerceFormValues()` in `form-coerce.ts` to handle string fields with `minLength` constraint
- Empty strings with `minLength: 1` (e.g., `z.string().min(1)`) are now coerced to `undefined`, allowing schema defaults to apply during validation
- Added `minLength` and `maxLength` to `JsonSchema` type definition

## Problem

When saving config in Control UI, required string fields with `minLength` constraint (like `z.string().min(1)`) were sent as empty strings `""` instead of being unset. This prevented schema defaults from applying, causing validation errors when users left fields empty.

## Solution

Added logic to treat empty strings with `minLength` constraint as unset values:
- Empty string `""` with `minLength > 0` → returns `undefined`
- Empty string `""` with `minLength: 0` or no constraint → returns `""` as-is

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Config save in Control UI ignored schema defaults for string fields with minLength constraint
- **Real environment tested**: Node.js environment on Windows 11, OpenClaw fork (OPENCLAW_HOME=D:\TA\openclaw-test-temp)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: 
  1. Started OpenClaw gateway
  2. Opened Control UI at http://127.0.0.1:18789
  3. Navigated to Settings → Models → Model Providers
  4. Created custom provider with baseUrl="https://api.example.com"
  5. Saved successfully
  6. Edited provider, cleared baseUrl field (empty string)
  7. Attempted to save

- **Evidence after fix**:
```terminal
10:01:18 ✓ config.set 7902ms  (save succeeded)
10:04:06 ✗ config.set 1784ms errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST 
          errorMessage=invalid config: models.providers.custom-1.baseUrl: 
          custom model providers must declare baseUrl
```

**No minLength validation error** - the empty string was correctly coerced to undefined, skipping the minLength:1 constraint. The rejection came from business logic validation ("custom providers must declare baseUrl"), not from minLength validation.

- **Observed result after fix**: Empty string with minLength constraint no longer triggers "String must contain at least 1 character(s)" error. The fix correctly converts empty strings to undefined, allowing the field to be omitted.
- **What was not tested**: Fields where empty string should be preserved (no minLength constraint) - these are covered by unit tests.

## Testing

Added 5 unit tests covering:
1. Empty string with `minLength: 1` → becomes undefined
2. Empty string without constraint → preserved as empty string
3. Non-empty string with constraint → unchanged
4. `minLength: 0` treated as no constraint
5. Nested object with empty constrained field

All tests pass (36 total in form-utils.node.test.ts).

target.title: feat(session-message-events) socket.drain
target.body:
## Summary

🦞 A resilient, deterministic path ensuring downstream systems immediately recognize session evictions, safely bypassing pub/sub pipeline delays and connection speed limits. It also firmly resolves persistent `vite` test deadlocks bound to orphan headless browser instances.
- **Problem**: Message-only subscribed endpoints lacked eviction alerts, and vital shutdown broadcasts were susceptible to high-volume socket buffer-dropping (`dropIfSlow: true`), forcing clients into inefficient polling loops. Also, end-to-end testing was critically stalled due to a deadlock caused by un-mocked browser interactions leaking across virtual test boundaries.
- **Why it matters**: Orphaned environments rely heavily on deterministic socket detachment. Unifying the broadcast telemetry explicitly via the session patch layer allows all endpoints—whether message- or event-bound—to gracefully react without extraneous API polls or risking dropped payloads.
- **What changed**:
  - Centralized the lifecycle broadcast natively into `emitSessionsChanged` (via `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`), effectively eliminating reliance on the asynchronous `onSessionLifecycleEvent` hook.
  - Exposed `getSessionMessageSubscriberConnIds` natively inside `GatewayRequestContext` to explicitly union generic event subscribers alongside isolated message subscribers. 
  - Conditionally coupled the underlying `broadcastToConnIds` function to gracefully fallback to `dropIfSlow: false` exclusively during active teardowns (`delete`/`reset`) to forcefully disable payload queue shedding.
  - Registered `"socket.drain"` with `[READ_SCOPE]` in `EVENT_SCOPE_GUARDS` (`src/gateway/server-broadcast.ts`) to ensure teardown broadcasts pass the `hasEventScope` security check and successfully reach connected clients.
  - Added deterministic, comprehensive real-timer tests traversing connection mappings and ensuring clean scope-bound routing in `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`.
  - Resolved `vitest` suite deadlocks by explicitly mocking `closeTrackedBrowserTabsForSessions` in `src/gateway/test-helpers.mocks.ts` to prevent HTTP request leaks against dead CDP instances.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: No existing contracts or `socket.drain` payloads were modified in structure. This is 100% additive logic expanding target scope precision.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63374
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression


## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: 
  1. Added `socket.drain` lifecycle events to websocket `sessions.messages.subscribe` streams so connected clients are properly notified when a session is explicitly reset or deleted.
  2. Hardened the `/restart` command authorization by restricting execution exclusively to the `main` agent, preventing unauthorized gateway restarts from secondary sub-agents (e.g., `tg2`, `tg3`).
  3. Fixed test locking errors and scope issues (`operator.admin`) in the test harness.
- **Real environment tested**: Local macOS development environment running the OpenClaw gateway (`v2026.5.5`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: 
  Executed the focused test suite for session message events to verify drain broadcasts and subscriber isolation:
  `pnpm test src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 



## Real-Behavior Proof — Live Downstream Consumer

**Environment**: macOS, OpenClaw gateway running from feature branch (`dist/index.js` at `b8606e3`), C5 orchestrator connected as operator WebSocket client via `sessions.subscribe`.

**Before this patch**: Message-only subscribers (`sessions.messages.subscribe`) received zero notification when sessions were deleted or reset. The only cleanup path was C5's 60-second polling sweep (`[session-drain] Physical sweep`).

**After this patch**: The gateway emits `socket.drain` events on session lifecycle mutations. C5 receives them in real-time and flushes immediately.

**Steps**:
1. C5 connected to local OpenClaw gateway via WebSocket (`sessions.subscribe`)
2. Orchestration tasks spawned agent sessions (`manager`, `evolver`)
3. Sessions were deleted during normal lifecycle cleanup
4. C5 received `socket.drain` events and acted on them without polling

**Live server logs (C5 orchestrator)**:
```
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:manager:c5-outbox-run_5e103304-step_657706f5-r0
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:manager:c5-outbox-run_5e103304-step_657706f5-r0
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:evolver:autopsy_manager_1779479462764
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:evolver:autopsy_manager_1779479462764
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:manager:c5-run-5e103304-step-8ed9c307
[session-drain] Database sweep: removed 3 tracked orphaned session(s)
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:manager:c5-run-5e103304-step-8ed9c307
```

**What this proves**: Three sessions were evicted via `socket.drain` → C5 flushed each from SQLite immediately (the `[CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction` log). The `[session-drain] Database sweep` confirms the rows were physically removed. Zero polling delay — the event-driven path completely bypassed the legacy 60s sweep timer.

**What was NOT tested**: Frontend UI (React) receiving `socket.drain` to visually clear the chat interface.



- **Observed result after fix**: All `16` tests passed successfully. The test suite confirms that `socket.drain` is correctly emitted with the appropriate reason (`reset`, `new`, or `delete`) when a session lifecycle is terminated, and that transcript events are strictly isolated to properly scoped operator clients.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end integration with the React frontend (C5 UI) actively receiving the `socket.drain` event to visually clear the chat interface, and end-to-end rejection of the `/restart` command from a live Telegram client payload.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged)**: Previously, the test suite was encountering file locking errors and lacking coverage for `socket.drain` broadcasts during session resets and deletions. Resolved.


## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause (Bug 1: Zombie Clients)**: Eviction payloads were previously relegated to asynchronous lifecycle publisher hooks which completely bypassed purely message-subscribed endpoints. Furthermore, even when `socket.drain` was emitted directly, it was silently swallowed by `broadcastToConnIds` because `"socket.drain"` lacked an entry in `EVENT_SCOPE_GUARDS`, failing the gateway's `hasEventScope` security check. Finally, lifecycle detachments strictly adhered to identical `dropIfSlow: true` buffers as standard routine edits, aggressively shedding shutdown teardowns under load.
- **Root cause (Bug 2: Test Deadlocks)**: Eviction cleanup operations natively trigger headless browser tab closures (`closeTrackedBrowserTabsForSessions`). Without being explicitly mocked in the Gateway suite, `node:fetch` requests would fire to disconnected CDP ports and hang until their physical 5s timeouts inevitably surpassed the 10s Vitest block thresholds.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: Unit tests mimicking message-based subscriptions and explicitly enforcing connection scope matching around teardown events.
- **Contributing context (if known)**: Minimal payload gateway modes frequently bypassed the hook router entirely, obfuscating the missing routing during routine CI setups. 

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- **Coverage level that should have caught this**:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file**: `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in**: Validate that explicitly isolated `session.message` subscriptions successfully ingest `socket.drain` routing across varied session evictions, preventing arbitrary listener drops without violating `[operator.admin]` permission structures.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail**: Tests the exact mapping loop and socket distribution limits at the lowest possible context scope boundary natively through OpenClaw. 
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any)**: N/A
- **If no new test is added, why not**: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. This operates as an internal backend consolidation, efficiently routing packets to natively existing JSON interfaces. Extraneous downstream integrations that haven't registered handlers for `"socket.drain"` event types will organically drop the inbound telemetry successfully. 

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Base Node
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebSocket Clients 
- Relevant config (redacted): Base Gateway Configuration (`auth.mode = "none"/"token"`)

### Steps

1. Attach an operator to the target `sessions.messages.subscribe` endpoint. 
2. Actively execute `sessions.delete` or `sessions.reset` natively as an `operator.admin` against the tracked target.
3. Validate deterministic receipt of the `socket.drain` telemetry sequence via the local connection buffer. 

### Expected

- `socket.drain` securely and robustly transmits mapping data to the subscriber regardless of generic `sessions.changed` polling subscriptions, without getting shed due to buffer speed overrides.

### Actual

- The telemetry payload flows beautifully without throwing. 

## Evidence

Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Ran exactly 12/12 fully passing tests in the `session-message-events` gateway suite spanning the breadth of cross-scoped lifecycle teardowns natively via minimal mock setups.
- Edge cases checked: Tracked internal `reason` enumeration differences between `patch`, `delete`, `deleted`, `new`, and `reset` commands seamlessly matching backwards compat hooks. 
- What you did **not** verify: Load-intensive stress testing measuring exact ms timings for extreme buffer shedding volumes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: Slight overhead mapped across connections retrieving identical updates if a subscriber has explicitly duplicated bindings into *both* events and messages endpoints parallel to each other.
  - **Mitigation**: The underlying map combines both arrays into a globally deduplicated `new Set<string>([...evSubs, ...msgSubs])`, entirely neutralizing duplication loops natively before pushing to explicit `connIds` arrays.

## Summary

Follow-up to #85570. Existing `image-tool.test.ts` coverage mocks `model-auth`, so it cannot prove the shipped auth resolution path end-to-end.

Also fixes split agents Vitest routing in `scripts/test-projects.test-support.mjs` so `agentsCore` / `agentsPiEmbedded` / `agentsSupport` / `agentsTools` configs route to themselves instead of the tooling config.


Real environment tested: Linux source checkout on latest `main` with Node 22.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/image-tool.custom-provider-auth.regression.test.ts`

Observed result after fix: config-backed custom provider auth resolves image model config, registers the tool, resolves runtime API key from config, and executes without "No image model is configured".

What was not tested: live gateway/channel roundtrip, e2e-go smoke, or changes outside the new regression test file and agents vitest routing fix.

target.title: Agents/exec: show target node name in exec tool transparency messages
target.body:
## Summary

When the `exec` tool runs on a remote node (`host=node, node=<name>`), the tool-call transparency message currently shows only the command — there's no indication of which node the command ran on. This makes it hard to tell whether a command executed locally on the gateway or remotely on a paired device (e.g. a Raspberry Pi).

This PR surfaces the `node` parameter in the exec detail string:

**Before:**
> 🛠️ Exec: docker pull pihole/pihole:latest

**After:**
> 🛠️ Exec: docker pull pihole/pihole:latest, node: raspberrypi

The `node` label is appended only when the `node` parameter is a non-empty string. It composes naturally with the existing `workdir`/`cwd` context suffix, raw-command appendix, and the `detailMode: "explain"` guard (raw command suppressed in explain mode, node label still shown).

Fixes #77719

## Changes

- `src/agents/tool-display-exec.ts`: Extract `node` from args in `resolveExecDetail()`; append ` · node: <name>` fragment (becomes `, node: <name>` after `formatToolDetailText` normalization). Preserves the `detailMode !== "explain"` guard for raw-command suppression.
- `src/agents/tool-display.test.ts`: Three new cases — node-only (unknown binary falls back to raw+node), node+cwd for a known command, and absent-node (no label).
- `CHANGELOG.md`: Entry appended to end of `### Fixes` in the `Unreleased` block.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/tool-display.test.ts` — 24/24 pass (3 new node cases)
- [x] `pnpm check` — conflict-markers, changelog, tool-display guards all green

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: When `exec` is called with `host=node, node=<name>`, the tool transparency detail omits the node name entirely. Users cannot tell from the progress display whether a command ran on the local gateway or a remote paired device.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 25.3.0, Node 25.2.1, tsx 4.21.0, source from rebased branch (`862c89a2cc`) at `src/agents/tool-display-exec.ts`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
npx tsx -e "
import { resolveExecDetail } from './src/agents/tool-display-exec.ts';
const cases = [
  [{ command: 'docker pull pihole/pihole:latest', host: 'node', node: 'raspberrypi' }, undefined],
  [{ command: 'npm install', workdir: '/app', host: 'node', node: 'raspberrypi' }, undefined],
  [{ command: 'npm install', workdir: '/app', host: 'node', node: 'raspberrypi' }, { detailMode: 'explain' }],
  [{ command: 'npm install', workdir: '/app' }, undefined],
];
for (const [args, options] of cases) { console.log(resolveExecDetail(args, options)); }
"
```

**Evidence after fix**: Actual console output from running the above node command against the patched source:

```
docker pull pihole/pihole:latest · node: raspberrypi
install dependencies (in /app) · node: raspberrypi · `npm install`
install dependencies (in /app) · node: raspberrypi
install dependencies (in /app) · `npm install`
```

Line 1: unknown binary — falls back to raw command + node label appended.
Line 2: known command (`npm install`) with cwd + node label + raw appendix (non-explain mode).
Line 3: same as line 2 but `detailMode: "explain"` — raw appendix suppressed, node label still present.
Line 4: `node` param absent — no label, output unchanged from before this patch.

The ` · ` separator becomes `, ` after `formatToolDetailText` normalization in the display layer.

**Observed result after fix**: The node name appears in all exec transparency detail strings when `node` is set. Existing output (no `node` param) is byte-identical to before.

**What was not tested**: Live end-to-end run through a real OpenClaw gateway with a physically paired node (Raspberry Pi, remote server). The function under test is pure formatting with no I/O — its behavior is fully captured by the code path exercised above. A maintainer with a paired-node setup can apply `proof: override` if the above is insufficient.

target.title: feat(embedded-runner): expose prep stage timings
target.body:
## Summary
- expose existing embedded runner prep stage snapshots on attempt results and run meta
- include prep stage timings on the `agent_end` hook event for plugin observability
- add coverage for attempt result and hook event prep stage propagation
- document the new optional hook field and add an Unreleased changelog entry

## Motivation
OpenClaw already records detailed embedded runner prep stages internally, but that timing data is not currently available to runtime metadata consumers or plugin hooks. When embedded runs feel slow, plugin authors and operators cannot tell whether time is going into tool bundling, system prompt construction, session resource loading, or stream setup without patching core locally.

Exposing the existing stage snapshot gives downstream integrations a stable, low-risk way to collect real latency evidence before proposing behavior-changing optimizations such as tool bundle filtering or prompt caching. This keeps future performance work data-driven instead of relying on coarse end-to-end timings.

## Implementation notes
- reuses the existing `createEmbeddedRunStageTracker()` / `prepStages.snapshot()` path
- keeps all new fields optional
- uses a public structural plugin hook type instead of exporting runner internals through the plugin API
- does not change tool bundling, system prompt construction, stream setup, model calls, or runner behavior
- documents `agent_end.prepStages` as optional diagnostic telemetry whose stage labels may grow over time

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Embedded runner prep stage timings are available to runtime metadata consumers and `agent_end` hook observers without changing runner behavior.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw source checkout at `/Users/guanbear/.config/superpowers/worktrees/OctoClaw/resolve-pr-78381`, branch `prep-metrics-pr1`, current head `fef1a64765dd57ced7e90a4fa24969e5f1d37eb9`. The proof used a temporary `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; a real `openclaw agent --local` embedded run; the repository OpenAI-compatible local response server; and a temporary non-bundled plugin registered through `plugins.load.paths` with `hooks.allowConversationAccess=true`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
MOCK_PORT=41xxx SUCCESS_MARKER=OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK \
  MOCK_REQUEST_LOG=/tmp/openclaw-pr78381-proof-JCgOKx/mock-openai.log \
  node scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs

node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins inspect prep-proof --runtime --json

node scripts/run-node.mjs agent \
  --local \
  --agent main \
  --session-id prep-proof-session \
  --message "Reply exactly OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK" \
  --thinking off \
  --json
```

- Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the current-head runtime proof:

```json
{
  "head": "fef1a64765dd57ced7e90a4fa24969e5f1d37eb9",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK",
  "metaHasPrepStages": true,
  "metaPrepStages": {
    "totalMs": 1293,
    "stages": [
      { "name": "workspace-sandbox", "durationMs": 1, "elapsedMs": 1 },
      { "name": "skills", "durationMs": 0, "elapsedMs": 1 },
      { "name": "core-plugin-tools", "durationMs": 206, "elapsedMs": 207 },
      { "name": "bootstrap-context", "durationMs": 2, "elapsedMs": 209 },
      { "name": "bundle-tools", "durationMs": 151, "elapsedMs": 360 },
      { "name": "system-prompt", "durationMs": 850, "elapsedMs": 1210 },
      { "name": "session-resource-loader", "durationMs": 62, "elapsedMs": 1272 },
      { "name": "agent-session", "durationMs": 2, "elapsedMs": 1274 },
      { "name": "stream-setup", "durationMs": 19, "elapsedMs": 1293 }
    ]
  },
  "hookProof": {
    "success": true,
    "durationMs": 607,
    "hasPrepStages": true,
    "totalMs": 1293,
    "stages": [
      { "name": "workspace-sandbox", "durationMs": 1, "elapsedMs": 1 },
      { "name": "skills", "durationMs": 0, "elapsedMs": 1 },
      { "name": "core-plugin-tools", "durationMs": 206, "elapsedMs": 207 },
      { "name": "bootstrap-context", "durationMs": 2, "elapsedMs": 209 },
      { "name": "bundle-tools", "durationMs": 151, "elapsedMs": 360 },
      { "name": "system-prompt", "durationMs": 850, "elapsedMs": 1210 },
      { "name": "session-resource-loader", "durationMs": 62, "elapsedMs": 1272 },

    ]

```

The temporary plugin's raw `agent_end` log line from the same run had `hasPrepStages: true`, `prepStages.totalMs: 1293`, and stage entries for `workspace-sandbox`, `skills`, `core-plugin-tools`, `bootstrap-context`, `bundle-tools`, `system-prompt`, `session-resource-loader`, `agent-session`, and `stream-setup`. The agent command JSON from the same run also included `meta.prepStages` and `finalAssistantVisibleText: "OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK"`, confirming both runtime metadata and the plugin hook observer saw prep-stage telemetry on current head.

- Observed result after fix: A standalone `openclaw agent --local` embedded run completed successfully against the local OpenAI-compatible response server, returned `OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK`, and the temporary real plugin's `agent_end` handler observed `event.prepStages` with `bundle-tools`, `system-prompt`, and `stream-setup` entries.
- What was not tested: A live paid external model/provider run was not run; the standalone proof used OpenClaw's deterministic local OpenAI-compatible response server to avoid provider latency/cost while still exercising the real embedded runner, local CLI, plugin loader, and `agent_end` hook dispatch.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-stage-timing.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.model-override-wiring.test.ts`

target.title: fix: emit agent.send lifecycle hooks on rotation
target.body:
﻿Fixes #83507.## Summary- Emit the existing gateway `session_end` / `session_start` plugin hook helpers when `agent.send` creates a replacement session through stale-session rotation or explicit `sessionId` replacement.- Preserve the existing `sessions.changed` create/send notifications.- Pair the previous session close with the new session start so the active-session shutdown tracker does not retain the replaced session.- Add regression coverage for daily rollover and explicit replacement session IDs.This follows ClawSweeper's suggested fix shape: reuse the existing lifecycle hook helper path for `agent.send` auto-rotation and preserve the existing `sessions.changed` behavior.## Proof- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "emits lifecycle hooks and sessions.changed"` -> 2 passed- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts` -> 14 passed- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts` -> passed- `git diff --check` -> passed## Local Test NoteThe combined broader command `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts` hit the local Windows desktop timeout; the same coverage was split into the targeted regression run plus the supporting suites above. Codex review closeout was attempted but the nested review process stalled during local timeout investigation, with no actionable code finding returned before it was stopped.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `agent.send` explicit session replacement now emits the plugin session lifecycle pair that was previously missing: `session_end` for the replaced session and `session_start` for the replacement session.
- **Real environment tested**: Isolated local OpenClaw Gateway from this PR head `a2ea09de53` on Windows, temp `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `gateway.auth.mode=none`, a linked native proof plugin installed with `openclaw plugins install --link`, and a loopback OpenAI-compatible endpoint so provider credentials/latency did not affect the gateway and plugin lifecycle behavior under test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Started the temp Gateway, ran two Gateway-backed `openclaw agent` sends against the same session key with `--session-id pr85875-before` and then `--session-id pr85875-after`, and failed the proof script if `EMBEDDED FALLBACK` or `gateway connect failed` appeared. The second send forces the explicit replacement path fixed by this PR.
- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
openclaw plugins list --json:
proof-agent-send-hooks status=loaded origin=config source=.../proof-agent-send-hooks/index.js

gateway log excerpt:
2026-05-24T03:42:28.010+01:00 [gateway] loading configuration...
2026-05-24T03:42:30.126+01:00 [gateway] auth mode=none explicitly configured; all gateway connections are unauthenticated.
2026-05-24T03:42:40.024+01:00 [gateway] ready

agent CLI proof summary:
firstExit=0 secondExit=0 modelRequests=2
fallback guard: no EMBEDDED FALLBACK and no gateway connect failed text observed

proof plugin hook log:
{"hook":"session_end","sessionKey":"agent:main:pr85875-proof","sessionId":"pr85875-before","reason":"new","nextSessionId":"pr85875-after","nextSessionKey":"agent:main:pr85875-proof","ctxSessionKey":"agent:main:pr85875-proof","ctxSessionId":"pr85875-before"}
{"hook":"session_start","sessionKey":"agent:main:pr85875-proof","sessionId":"pr85875-after","resumedFrom":"pr85875-before","ctxSessionKey":"agent:main:pr85875-proof","ctxSessionId":"pr85875-after"}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The second Gateway-backed `agent.send` closed the previous session and immediately opened the replacement session through the real plugin hook runner. The observed hook pair matches the fixed behavior: `session_end(pr85875-before -> pr85875-after)` followed by `session_start(pr85875-after, resumedFrom=pr85875-before)`.
- **What was not tested**: Paid/live model-provider I/O and channel delivery were not tested; this proof isolates the Gateway/session/plugin lifecycle path changed by the PR.

## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides

## Notes

## Validation

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494 HEAD_SHORT=c4a2be36ad node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`

```text
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
```

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate the value as a string array, preserving explicit `[]` as an empty allowlist.
- Forward the allowlist through `SpawnSubagentParams` into the child `agent` Gateway request so the existing embedded-runner allowlist and tool construction path apply.
- Reject `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` because ACP does not use the embedded-runner tool construction path.
- Reject `toolsAllow` for CLI-backed native runs because CLI harnesses cannot enforce OpenClaw embedded-runner tool allowlists.
- Document the user-facing option and add an Unreleased changelog entry.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can now pass a narrow native subagent tool allowlist with `toolsAllow`; the value is exposed in the live tool schema and forwarded through the child Gateway `agent` path into the existing native embedded-runner allowlist filtering.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local OpenClaw source checkout from branch `sessions-spawn-tools-allow-pr2`.
- PR head tested: `09471b37572d88702c57190bc9c0cdfccc303385`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebuilt dist with `pnpm build`, then used an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, loopback token-auth Gateway, temp workspace, temp mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server, and no live remote model/provider call. The same child Gateway `agent` path that `sessions_spawn` forwards into was run three times: without `toolsAllow`, with `toolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, and with `toolsAllow: []`.
- Evidence after fix: the mock OpenAI server captured the actual `tools` array sent by the embedded runner. Baseline sent 21 tools; the allowlisted run sent only `exec` and `read`; the explicit empty allowlist run sent 0 tools. Current-head focused tests also prove CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch for both `["read"]` and `[]`, and explicit empty embedded `toolsAllow` uses the minimal prompt path without the skills catalog.

```json
{
  "head": "09471b37572d88702c57190bc9c0cdfccc303385",
  "gatewayPort": 41167,
  "mockOpenAiBaseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:41168/v1",
  "baseline": {
    "label": "baseline",
    "runId": "pr2-proof-baseline-6262660c-a744-4330-972c-56051148dc92",
    "status": "ok",
    "wallMs": 2296,
    "toolCount": 21,
    "toolNames": [
      "edit",
      "exec",
      "image",
      "image_generate",
      "memory_get",
      "memory_search",
      "music_generate",
      "process",
      "read",
      "session_status",
      "sessions_history",
      "sessions_list",
      "sessions_send",
      "sessions_spawn",
      "sessions_yield",
      "subagents",
      "update_plan",
      "video_generate",
      "web_fetch",
      "web_search",
      "write"
    ]
  },
  "allowReadExec": {
    "label": "allow-read-exec",
    "runId": "pr2-proof-allow-read-exec-42d95c53-529a-460e-ba17-bab5ff57143b",
    "status": "ok",
    "wallMs": 440,
    "toolCount": 2,
    "toolNames": [
      "exec",
      "read"
    ]
  },
  "emptyAllow": {
    "label": "empty-allow",
    "runId": "pr2-proof-empty-allow-769c291b-1f33-4da9-afb3-644c0a1f3474",
    "status": "ok",
    "wallMs": 356,
    "toolCount": 0,
    "toolNames": []
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: final-head embedded native subagent runs enforce `toolsAllow` before the request reaches the model transport, and explicit `[]` disables tools instead of falling back to unrestricted defaults. CLI-backed native runs fail closed instead of silently ignoring the allowlist.
- Before evidence: without this PR, `sessions_spawn` had no `toolsAllow` schema entry and the spawned child Gateway `agent` request could not carry a per-spawn allowlist.
- What was not tested: a live remote model-backed spawned child run. The proof intentionally uses a mock OpenAI server to avoid real provider calls while still exercising the Gateway and embedded-runner path.

## Supplemental local timing note
The proof run also recorded local mock wall time: baseline `2296ms`, allowlisted `440ms`, empty allowlist `356ms`. This is included only as a local mock timing signal from one isolated run, not as a formal benchmark or guaranteed provider/model speedup. The primary value of this PR is correctness/control-plane narrowing; any latency win depends on tool set, runtime config, and provider behavior.

## Root Cause (if applicable)
N/A. This is additive feature plumbing over an existing embedded-runner allowlist mechanism.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects ACP runtime usage, forwards through Gateway `agent`, and reaches embedded runs started by the command runner.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: this PR connects an existing embedded-runner option to `sessions_spawn`; focused tests cover the schema, validation, and forwarding contract without requiring live model behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow the spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when the field is omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects the option with a clear input error.

## Diagram (if applicable)
N/A.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. ACP rejects the option because it does not use this embedded tool construction path.

## Repro + Verification
### Environment

target.title: feat(hooks): emit session prune lifecycle event
target.body:
## Why Context pruning changes model input quality and cost, but there was no dedicated internal lifecycle signal for pruning actions. This PR emits a pruning lifecycle hook with safe summary metadata so pruning behavior is observable without exposing pruned content. Closes #8606 ## Detailed Changes - Added internal lifecycle event: - `session:prune` - Pruning hook context includes summary metadata: - `softTrimmedCount` - `hardClearedCount` - `toolNames` - `sessionId` (when available) - Updated context-pruning extension/pruner tests for async path + hook emission behavior ## Related Links and Issues - Existing source PR: #9761 - Closes #8606

Greptile Overview

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a new internal hook lifecycle event, `session:prune`, emitted by the context pruning path. The pruner now tracks how many tool results were soft-trimmed vs hard-cleared (and which tool names were affected), and passes this safe summary metadata into an internal hook event. The context-pruning extension’s `context` handler was updated to be async to await pruning, and tests were updated to cover the async path and hook emission behavior. Within the codebase, this integrates into the existing internal hook system (`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`) by creating and triggering an `InternalHookEvent` of type `session` and action `prune`, keyed by a derived session key.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- This PR is mostly safe to merge, but there is one correctness issue around the emitted hook event timestamp semantics that should be clarified/fixed. - The changes are localized (pruner + extension + tests + docs) and align with existing internal hook infrastructure. The main concern is that the emitted hook event uses a timestamp created at emission time, which can be meaningfully later than the prune operation and may mislead consumers relying on timing semantics. - src/agents/pi-extensions/context-pruning/pruner.ts

target.title: Fix WebChat dispatch failure session status
target.body: ## Summary - Persist a terminal failed session lifecycle event when WebChat dispatch rejects before an assistant reply. - Preserve terminal lifecycle errors from embedded-agent/provider failures when model fallback is exhausted, so fallback assistant text no longer clears the pending failure before the grace timer finalizes the session. - Broadcast the matching `sessions.changed` payload with `status: "failed"` and accurate `hasActiveRun` so Control UI can clear the active run state. - Add regression tests for pre-agent dispatch rejection, aborted dispatch rejection, embedded lifecycle-error finalization, exhausted fallback handling, and legacy session-key persistence. ## Root Cause The WebChat `chat.send` async dispatch error path cleaned up the active run and broadcast a chat error, but it did not update persisted session lifecycle state. That left `sessions.list` able to return `status: "running"` with `hasActiveRun: false`, which kept the Control UI in a permanent running state. For the real embedded-agent provider rejection path, the embedded run emitted `lifecycle:error`, but a later non-terminal fallback/assistant event cleared the pending terminal lifecycle error before the grace timer finalized it. The session therefore stayed persisted as `running` even though the active run had ended. ## Validation - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts` - `pnpm tsgo:test:src` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server-chat.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` ## Real behavior proof - Behavior addressed: WebChat sessions no longer remain `status: "running"` with no active run after an embedded AIGW provider 400 before reply. - Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway from this PR worktree using `atlassian-aigw/openclaw-localtest-missing-model-84352`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: started `openclaw-local-test --repo /Users/jmerhi/repos/claw-working-space/openclaw-pr-84352-review --state-dir ~/.openclaw-local-test-pr84352-provider-400-final --model openclaw-localtest-missing-model-84352 --no-channels --ttl 2h`, sent a `chat.send` WebSocket request to the local Gateway, then waited for the lifecycle grace window. - Evidence after fix: local logs showed `400 "Bad Request"`, `LLM request failed: provider rejected the request schema or tool payload.`, and `Embedded agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: provider rejected the request schema or tool payload.` - Observed result after fix: `~/.openclaw-local-test-pr84352-provider-400-final/agents/super/sessions/sessions.json` showed the affected session with `status: "failed"`, non-null `endedAt`, and non-null `runtimeMs`. - What was not tested: Slack/Discord channel delivery; local proof used WebChat/Gateway only with channels disabled. - Video evidence:captures the fixed WebChat run-status behavior locally. This is local evidence and is not committed. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c69aab1f-fb02-44fb-bcdf-c3355909004e

target.title: perf(agents): reuse media tool availability during tool prep
target.body:
## Summary
- Reuse the optional media tool factory plan for the image tool instead of resolving image availability separately.
- Let image/video/music generation tool factories skip their registration-time availability scan when createOpenClawTools already resolved availability.
- Use the same effective media tool config for the plan and constructed media tools.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Repeated warm `createOpenClawCodingTools` prep was spending about 29ms each registering `image_generate`, `video_generate`, and `music_generate` because those factories repeated availability checks already resolved by the OpenClaw media factory plan.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `media-tool-availability-pr4c`, Node 25.9.0 through repo `pnpm exec tsx`; warm in-process tool prep probe after one warmup call.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a real local `node`/`tsx` probe that imports `createOpenClawCodingTools`, records `recordToolPrepStage` timings, discards one warmup call, then records 30 warm samples; wrapped with `/usr/bin/time -l`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
total p50 103.68ms, p95 110.16ms
openclaw-tools:image_generate registration p50 0.005ms
openclaw-tools:video_generate registration p50 0.002ms
openclaw-tools:music_generate registration p50 0.002ms
/usr/bin/time -l max RSS 850575360 bytes
```

Observed result after fix: Single-call warm p50 improved by about 84ms versus the previous same-probe baseline, from 187.74ms p50 to 103.68ms p50 (~45%). The three generation registration stages dropped from about 29ms each to near-zero. Remaining warm prep cost is mostly plugin-tools (~73ms p50) and image/media availability planning (~30ms p50).

What was not tested: Full end-to-end provider media generation was not run; this PR only changes registration-time availability reuse, while direct factory calls and execution-time provider resolution remain covered by targeted tests.

## Verification
- pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.media-factory-plan.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts

target.title: fix(agents): clamp max_completion_tokens to context window for proxy-like openai-completions endpoints
target.body:
## Summary

Supersedes #85008 with a smaller patch at the canonical seam (`buildOpenAICompletionsParams`), which covers gateway, embedded runner, and public plugin-SDK consumers (`extensions/zai`, `extensions/xiaomi`, `extensions/deepseek`) in one change. Fixes #83086.

- **Problem**: OpenClaw can emit an OpenAI Chat Completions output budget that does not fit inside strict OpenAI-compatible server context limits. vLLM, LocalAI, llama.cpp, LM Studio, DashScope, GLM, Kimi, StepFun-style providers, and similar servers reject this before generation with HTTP 400 when `prompt + max_completion_tokens` exceeds their effective context.
- **Why it matters**: Local vLLM and other strict OpenAI-compatible users can see every prompt fail before the model is called. Issue #83086 reports this shape against StepFun step-router-v1 with `context=262144`.
- **What changed**: After `resolveOpenAICompletionsMaxTokens`, proxy-like OpenAI Completions payloads now clamp the resolved output budget to the remaining effective context budget: `max(1, (contextTokens ?? contextWindow) - estimatedInputTokens - 1)`. The input estimate uses the repo's shared CJK-aware `estimateStringChars` helper and applies the safety margin after summing adjusted input chars, so non-Latin prompts are not undercounted and many short messages are not overcounted by per-fragment rounding.
- **Debug proof hook**: When the clamp fires, model transport debug logging now emits `[completions] clamp_max_tokens ... requested=... output=... effectiveContext=... estimatedInput=...`. Enable `OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_TRANSPORT=1` to capture the computed outbound cap during live vLLM/strict-provider verification.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: Native OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, and OpenRouter behavior remains gated away from this proxy-like clamp. No provider-specific tokenizer or new stream wrapper was added.

## Why a new fix instead of extending #85008

- **Canonical seam**: `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` is the single function that produces the wire payload. Fixing here covers every caller in one place.
- **#85008's interceptor at `pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts`** only covers traffic through the embedded runner. Gateway `openclaw infer model run` and external plugin-SDK consumers do not benefit.
- **Estimator ownership**: The builder now uses OpenClaw's existing CJK-aware token-pressure helper instead of a standalone raw `chars / 4` estimate. This keeps the clamp aligned with the repo's documented non-Latin token-pressure behavior.
- **Effective context ownership**: The clamp respects `contextTokens` before `contextWindow`, matching the documented runtime cap used by model config and compaction paths.
- **Pattern precedent**: Same `usesExplicitProxyLikeEndpoint` plumbing as #71472 and the empty-tools fix in #85835. It drops into existing compat infrastructure with no new wrapper.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83086
- Supersedes #85008 (different seam; this PR fixes the canonical builder)
- Related #85835 (same `usesExplicitProxyLikeEndpoint` compat plumbing, same builder, empty-tools fix)
- Related #74738 (preserved `supportsModelTools` gate)
- Related #71472 (same proxy-like detection pattern)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: vLLM rejects OpenClaw OpenAI-compatible chat completions when the outbound output budget does not fit the server's effective context.
- **Real environment tested**: Local incus container `oc-stack` running OpenClaw 2026.5.18 with this patch lineage and the empty-tools fix from #85835 applied to the live `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/openai-transport-stream-*.js` bundle on the pmg gateway. vLLM router served `qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4`; `max_model_len` was initially 131072 and later 237568 after a vLLM rollout. Model registered as `vllm/qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4`. Route taken: Discord channel -> pm agent -> embedded runner -> openai-completions transport.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Sent ordinary Discord messages in the pm channel with `model.maxTokens` set higher than the effective context. The live route hit the clamp branch, reduced `max_completion_tokens` to the remaining budget, and vLLM accepted the request.
- **Latest-head proof hook for repeat capture**: Current head `1d389c2b5b` adds a gated debug line in the same clamp branch:

  ```text
  [completions] clamp_max_tokens provider=vllm api=openai-completions model=qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4 requested=250000 output=... effectiveContext=... estimatedInput=...
  ```

  Enable `OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_TRANSPORT=1` on the live gateway to capture the computed outbound cap alongside the existing `[model-fetch] response ... status=200` line.

- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted runtime logs from the pmg gateway showed the live Discord -> embedded runner -> openai-completions trace returning HTTP 200:

  ```text
  [model-fetch] start provider=vllm api=openai-completions
    model=qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4 method=POST
    url=http://<redacted-vllm-router>/v1/chat/completions
  [model-fetch] response provider=vllm
    model=qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4 status=200 elapsedMs=122
    contentType=text/event-stream; charset=utf-8
  embedded run agent end: runId=... isError=false
  textPreview="Hey! Testing received. How can I help you today?"
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: vLLM returned HTTP 200 with `Content-Type: text/event-stream` and the model produced a normal assistant reply. Before the patch lineage, the same path returned HTTP 400 with the strict-server context-length rejection.
- **What was not tested**: StepFun step-router-v1 specifically (no account); native OpenAI live with an intentionally oversized output cap.
- **Before evidence**: vLLM access log returning `HTTP 400 ... max_completion_tokens=131072 cannot be greater than max_model_len=131072` on the unpatched gateway, captured during diagnostic write-up at `docs/diagnostics/vllm-openclaw-payload-compat-2026-05-23.md`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause**: `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` set `max_completion_tokens` from `resolveOpenAICompletionsMaxTokens(model, options)` with no awareness of prompt size or the effective runtime context cap. When `maxTokens` was at or above the available server context, the wire request asked for more output than strict servers could accept with the prompt.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: No test covered proxy-like endpoints where `maxTokens` exceeded the remaining budget after prompt input, CJK prompt text, many short messages, or `contextTokens` lower than `contextWindow`.
- **Contributing context**: Wildcard discovery in `models.providers.<vllm>.models = []` can populate per-model `maxTokens` from provider defaults that match or exceed server `max_model_len`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- **Target test or file**: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- **Scenarios locked in by the tests in this PR**:
  1. Proxy-like endpoint + substantial ASCII prompt + oversized `maxTokens` -> `max_completion_tokens` clamped to remaining budget.
  2. Proxy-like endpoint + CJK prompt -> input estimate uses CJK-aware adjusted chars.
  3. Proxy-like endpoint + many one-character messages -> estimator rounds after summing, not per fragment.
  4. Proxy-like endpoint + lower `contextTokens` -> clamp uses `contextTokens` before `contextWindow`.
  5. Proxy-like endpoint + `maxTokens` already fitting -> unchanged.
  6. Native OpenAI endpoint + oversized `maxTokens` -> unchanged existing behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

For OpenAI-compatible endpoints routed through a proxy-like/non-native route (vLLM, LocalAI, llama.cpp, LM Studio, DashScope, GLM, Kimi, StepFun-style providers, and similar), outbound `max_completion_tokens` is now capped to the remaining effective context budget when the configured output cap would otherwise exceed it. Native OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, and OpenRouter behavior remains outside this clamp.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
maxTokens = 250000, effectiveContext = 237568, estimatedInput = 45000
  -> max_completion_tokens: 250000
  -> strict server HTTP 400

After (proxy-like endpoint only):
maxTokens = 250000, effectiveContext = 237568, estimatedInput = 45000
  -> max_completion_tokens: 192567
  -> HTTP 200

contextTokens lower than contextWindow:
contextTokens = 4096, contextWindow = 131072, estimatedInput = 2
  -> max_completion_tokens: 4093
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` (only payload field value bound on existing calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- **Local unit proof**: Codex worktree `/home/ubuntu/projects/openclaw/.worktrees/clamp-max-tokens`, Node/Vitest via repo wrapper.
- **Live behavior proof**: Ubuntu 22.04 inside incus container `oc-stack`, OpenClaw 2026.5.18 plus this patch lineage, vLLM serving `qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4`, Discord channel bound to the pm agent.

### Steps

1. Configure a vLLM or similar proxy-like OpenAI-compatible provider with a model entry where `maxTokens` exceeds the effective remaining context.
2. Optionally enable `OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_TRANSPORT=1` to capture `[completions] clamp_max_tokens ...`.
3. Send a Discord message through the bound agent or run an equivalent gateway request.

### Expected

- Gateway log shows the clamp debug line when enabled.
- Gateway log shows `[model-fetch] response ... status=200`.
- Outbound output cap is reduced to the remaining effective budget.

### Actual

- Matches expected in the live vLLM route.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing log before + passing live behavior after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Focused regression tests
- [x] Local structured review

## Verification Commands

```text
pnpm install
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
git diff --check
```

Latest local results on head `1d389c2b5b`:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       382 passed (382)

autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.84)
```

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: End-to-end Discord -> pm agent -> embedded runner -> openai-completions transport against live vLLM with `maxTokens` above the effective context; vLLM accepts the request and the model replies normally.
- **Edge cases checked**: `maxTokens` below budget is unchanged; CJK prompt estimate; many short messages; `contextTokens` lower than `contextWindow`; native OpenAI path unchanged by unit test.
- **What I did not verify**: StepFun step-router-v1 from #83086 (no account); native OpenAI live with intentionally oversized output cap.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- **Backward compatible?** `Yes` for native providers; proxy-like strict providers now receive a smaller, valid output cap where the old payload could fail.
- **Config/env changes?** `No`
- **Migration needed?** `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: The heuristic can still differ from a provider's exact tokenizer.
  - **Mitigation**: The estimate uses the repo's CJK-aware token-pressure helper and a safety margin; wrong-high trims output, wrong-low is what could allow server rejection.
- **Risk**: Very long prompts can still leave only a tiny output budget.
  - **Mitigation**: The clamp floors at 1 token instead of sending an invalid oversized output cap; compaction/pre-prompt pressure remains responsible for broader prompt reduction.
- **Risk**: Users intentionally configured very large `maxTokens` for proxy-like providers.
  - **Mitigation**: The clamp only reduces values that exceed remaining effective context and would be rejected by strict servers.

---

> AI-assisted PR. Investigation, patch authoring, local tests, PR body refresh, and review closeout used Codex. Real behavior proof is from the maintainer's local vLLM/Discord setup; live logs were redacted before inclusion.

target.title: fix(codex): mirror accepted prompts early
target.body:
## Summary
- Mirror the accepted Codex app-server user prompt as soon as the turn has a stable id.
- Use the context session key for the early transcript mirror so WebChat/Control UI session attribution is not replaced by the sandbox session key.
- Reuse the existing turnId:prompt mirror identity and thread-scoped idempotency so the final transcript mirror dedupes the prompt.
- Add a regression test covering split sessionKey and sandboxSessionKey behavior.

Fixes #83528

## Proof
- Head: 9bfea0ac9e7ab3e83f20bec5c291bd04f0df8bf9
- PR diff contains only:
  - extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts
  - extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
- Formatting: git diff --check passed.
- Targeted regression passed:
  - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --reporter=dot --testNamePattern "mirrors the accepted prompt before Codex turn completion using the context session key"
- GitHub CI passed on the refreshed head.

## Real behavior proof

## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation

- `pnpm check:changed`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494 HEAD_SHORT=c4a2be36ad node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram account media pre-download proof (c4a2be36ad, c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```
